from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import udf, split

def get_issn_eissn(journal_url):
    try:
        # Fetch the HTML content
        response = requests.get(journal_url)
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, "html.parser")
       
        # Find the div containing the ISSN and eISSN
        div = soup.find("div", class_="overview__journal-owner-issn")
        # Extract ISSN and eISSN
        issn = div.find(string="ISSN:").find_next(string=True).strip() if div else None
        eissn = div.find(string="eISSN:").find_next(string=True).strip() if div else None
        return f"{issn}, {eissn}"
    except Exception as e:
        return None

def get_all_hybrid_journals():
    # Define schema for the DataFrame
    schema = StructType([
            StructField("journal_name", StringType(), True),
            StructField("journal_url", StringType(), True),
            StructField("price_GBP", StringType(), True),
            StructField("price_USD", StringType(), True),
            StructField("price_EUR", StringType(), True)
    ])

    response = requests.get(hybrid_journals_url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')

    oa_jouranl_table = soup.find("table")
    table_rows = oa_jouranl_table.find_all("tr")[1:]
    # Initialize an empty list to store extracted data
    data = []
    # Loop through the rows to extract journal data
    for row in table_rows:
        columns = row.find_all("td")
        journal_name = columns[0].find("a").text
        journal_url = columns[0].find("a")["href"]
        apc_gbp = columns[1].text
        apc_usd = columns[2].text
        apc_eur = columns[3].text
        data.append((journal_name, journal_url, apc_gbp, apc_usd, apc_eur))
    # Create the DataFrame
    journal_df = spark.createDataFrame(data, schema=schema)

    return journal_df

journal_df = get_all_hybrid_journals()
# Register the function as a UDF
get_issn_eissn_udf = udf(get_issn_eissn, StringType())
journal_df = journal_df.withColumn("ISSN_eISSN", get_issn_eissn_udf(journal_df["journal_url"]))
journal_df = journal_df.withColumn("issn", split(journal_df["ISSN_eISSN"], ", ")[0]) \
       .withColumn("eissn", split(journal_df["ISSN_eISSN"], ", ")[1]) \
       .drop("ISSN_eISSN")

def get_all_platinum_journals():


    response = requests.get(platinum_journals_url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')

    platinum_jouranl_table = soup.find("table")

    # Extract titles and ISSNs
    rows = platinum_jouranl_table.find_all("tr")[1:]  # Skip the header row
    data = []
    for row in rows:
        cols = row.find_all("td")
        
        # First column
        title_1 = cols[0].text.strip()
        link_1 = cols[0].find("a")
        url_1 = link_1["href"] if link_1 else None
        issn_1 = cols[1].text.strip()

        # Second column
        title_2 = cols[2].text.strip()
        link_2 = cols[2].find("a")
        url_2 = link_2["href"] if link_2 else None
        issn_2 = cols[3].text.strip()

        data.append((title_1, issn_1, url_1))
        data.append((title_2, issn_2, url_2))

    # Define the schema
    columns = ["Title", "ISSN", "URL"]

    # Create PySpark DataFrame
    df = spark.createDataFrame(data, columns)

    return df
